title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Cleaning Up: The Business of Graffiti Removal
content.text: At five o'clock on a Monday morning, before the city wakes, a small team of workers is already repainting a railway bridge in east London. By eight o'clock the last traces of an elaborate spray-painted mural will be gone. The graffiti removal industry operates in near-total anonymity, yet it plays a significant role in shaping the appearance of urban environments. (37) [37] Cities around the world spend hundreds of millions of pounds each year on removal and prevention programmes. [/37] || The work is more complex than it might appear. Different surfaces — brick, metal, glass, stone — require different chemical treatments and techniques. (38) [38] Applying the wrong solvent to a porous surface can drive paint deeper into the material, making removal even harder. [/38] Experienced technicians must assess each case individually, balancing speed with the need to avoid permanent damage. || The industry exists in an uncomfortable dialogue with the art world. Some graffiti — particularly the work of internationally recognised artists — commands enormous prices and is actively preserved by property owners. (39) [39] Deciding what to remove and what to protect requires aesthetic and cultural judgements that go far beyond simple property rights. [/39] In several cities, legal graffiti walls have been established as a compromise, giving artists a legitimate space to work. || Technology is changing the field. High-powered steam cleaners, laser ablation systems and new anti-graffiti coating formulations have all improved efficiency. (40) [40] These coatings, applied to walls after cleaning, create a surface from which paint can be removed much more easily in future. [/40] || Some municipalities have tried a different approach entirely: rapid response. Studies show that graffiti removed within 24 to 48 hours is far less likely to be repeated on the same surface. (41) [41] This 'quick response' strategy has been shown to reduce long-term removal costs significantly in cities that have adopted it. [/41] || The debate about graffiti — art or vandalism — is unlikely to be resolved soon. (42) [42] For now, the teams with their rollers and chemicals will continue their early-morning work, keeping a city clean one wall at a time. [/42]

paragraphs.A: The most significant recent innovation is the development of sacrificial anti-graffiti coatings.
paragraphs.B: In practice, however, the majority of unauthorised street art is removed without question.
paragraphs.C: The logic is that an unmarked, clean wall sends a signal that the area is actively maintained.
paragraphs.D: Professional training and specialist knowledge are therefore essential in this trade.
paragraphs.E: Meanwhile, the scale of the problem continues to grow in cities with expanding public transport networks.
paragraphs.F: Both sides of the argument have legitimate claims, and urban authorities must navigate carefully between them.
paragraphs.G: Chemical removers must be matched carefully to both the paint type and the underlying surface.

questions.explanation: E mentions that the problem grows in cities with expanding transport networks, supporting the scale of the problem introduced in the opening paragraph.
G explains that solvents must be adapted to the type of paint and to the surface, connecting with the idea that different surfaces require different treatments.
B notes that in practice the majority of street art is removed without question, contrasting with the cases of recognised artists that are preserved.
A introduces sacrificial anti-graffiti coatings as the most important innovation, detailing the type of coating mentioned just after.
C explains that a clean wall sends the signal that the area is maintained, justifying the logic behind the rapid response strategy.
F acknowledges the legitimate reasons on both sides of the debate, closing the article with a balanced perspective.
